sessions.title: Section 1 — Booking a Hotel Room
sessions.transcript:
[SPEAKER 1 — Receptionist]: Good afternoon, Lakeside Hotel. How can I help you?

[SPEAKER 2 — Customer]: Hi, yes, I'd like to make a booking for a room, please.

[SPEAKER 1]: Of course. Can I ask how many nights you'll be staying?

[SPEAKER 2]: Just two nights — the 14th and 15th of March.

[SPEAKER 1]: Lovely. And how many guests will there be?

[SPEAKER 2]: Three guests — two adults and one child.

[SPEAKER 1]: Perfect. We have a family room available on those dates. That would be £120 per night, so £240 in total. Would that work for you?

[SPEAKER 2]: Yes, that sounds fine. Does that include breakfast?

[SPEAKER 1]: It does include breakfast for all guests. We serve from 7am to 10am in the main dining room.

[SPEAKER 2]: Excellent. I'd also like to ask about parking — do you have spaces available?

[SPEAKER 1]: We do. Parking is free for all hotel guests. You just need to register your car registration number when you check in.

[SPEAKER 2]: That's great. Can I also book a table in the restaurant for Saturday evening?

[SPEAKER 1]: Certainly. What time would you prefer?

[SPEAKER 2]: Around 7 o'clock if possible.

[SPEAKER 1]: Let me check... yes, we have a table for three at 7pm on Saturday the 15th. I'll add that to your reservation.

[SPEAKER 2]: Thank you. Can I give you my name? It's Sarah Morgan — M-O-R-G-A-N.

[SPEAKER 1]: Thank you, Ms Morgan. Could I also take a contact number?

[SPEAKER 2]: Yes, it's 07854 231 967.

[SPEAKER 1]: And would you like to guarantee your booking with a credit card?

[SPEAKER 2]: Yes, the card number is 4872 3301 5588 9214.

[SPEAKER 1]: Thank you. Your booking is confirmed. We look forward to welcoming you on the 14th.

questions.question: The customer is booking for ___ nights.
The total cost of the stay is £___.
What is included in the room rate?
The restaurant table is booked for ___ pm on Saturday.

sessions.title: Timed Practice — Mixed Questions
sessions.transcript:
[SPEAKER 1 — Advisor]: Good morning. City Sports Centre, how can I help?

[SPEAKER 2 — Caller]: Morning. I'd like to find out about joining the gym, please.

[SPEAKER 1]: Of course. Are you interested in a monthly membership or an annual one?

[SPEAKER 2]: I'm not sure yet. Can you tell me the prices?

[SPEAKER 1]: Monthly membership is £45 per month. An annual membership works out cheaper — it's £420 for the year, so you'd save £120 compared to paying monthly.

[SPEAKER 2]: That's a good saving. What does the membership include?

[SPEAKER 1]: Full access to the gym floor, all group fitness classes, and free use of the swimming pool. We also have a sauna which is available to members at no extra cost.

[SPEAKER 2]: What are the opening hours?

[SPEAKER 1]: We're open Monday to Friday from 6am to 10pm, and on weekends from 8am to 8pm.

[SPEAKER 2]: Is there a joining fee?

[SPEAKER 1]: There's a one-off joining fee of £30, which covers your access card and an initial fitness assessment with one of our trainers.

[SPEAKER 2]: That sounds reasonable. Do you have parking?

[SPEAKER 1]: Yes, there's a car park directly behind the building. It's free for the first two hours, then £2 per hour after that.

[SPEAKER 2]: OK. And can I try the facilities before I commit?

[SPEAKER 1]: Absolutely. You can book a free trial day — just bring proof of address and a form of ID. Would you like me to book one for you?

[SPEAKER 2]: Yes please. How about this Thursday?

[SPEAKER 1]: Thursday the 20th — let me check... yes, that's available. Can I take your name?

[SPEAKER 2]: It's James Fletcher.

[SPEAKER 1]: Great, Mr Fletcher. Your trial day is booked for Thursday the 20th. We'll see you then.

questions.question: The annual membership saves members £___ compared to monthly payments.
Which of the following is NOT included in the membership?
On weekends, the centre opens at ___ am.
What does a visitor need to bring for a free trial day?
options: A. A doctor's note and ID
B. Proof of address and ID
C. A credit card and address
